segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens dramatically with a close-up of the Emirates FA Cup trophy, shrouded in smoke and illuminated by stadium lights, as a male commentator with a British accent declares it a "special occasion" to be cherished, questioning "Who will go down in history as the winners of the FA Cup?" Orchestral music builds intensity as the scene transitions to the exterior of Wembley Stadium, where excited fans in blue and red jerseys gather, with the stadium's large digital screen displaying 'WEMBLEY STADIUM' and FA Cup branding. Inside, the atmosphere intensifies with an upbeat electronic sports anthem as players from Manchester City, in light blue jerseys, and Manchester United, in red training jackets, walk onto the pitch, flanked by officials and the referee, who holds the official match ball. The commentator announces, "It's Manchester City, they face Manchester United, and it's live on EA TV." The teams line up for the pre-match ceremony, with Manchester City's Haaland (number 9) and Grealish (number 10) clearly visible. The scoreboard shows 0-0 at kickoff, and the commentator, Derek Rae, welcomes viewers to Wembley, joined by Stewart Robson, as they approach kickoff for what promises to be a "spectacular FA Cup final." Stewart Robson, with a nostalgic tone, reflects on the FA Cup final as the day "every young footballer dreamt of," playing in front of "100,000 fans at Wembley," a moment that "still brings a tingle to my spine." The massive crowd, a sea of red and blue, fills the stadium, their cheers audible, creating an electric ambiance as the players prepare for the game.

Manchester City, in light blue, and Manchester United, in red, are seen actively pressing and repositioning, with the match ball frequently heard with distinct kicks and thuds. The segment concludes with the commentator noting possession "given away by City," as the electric atmosphere and crowd energy persist, both teams relentlessly seeking to break the deadlock.

segments.integrated_caption: The Emirates FA Cup match between Manchester City and Manchester United is tied 2-2 as the segment begins, with the crowd's murmur and the sounds of the ball in play setting the scene. Manchester City, in light blue, and Manchester United, in red, are locked in a fast-paced midfield exchange. A commentator notes "Fred" and questions "what can they do from here?" as the ball moves towards City's attacking third. The tension builds as a commentator exclaims, "Could be a chance to break here!" and then, "Opportunity it is!" At 55:57, Riyad Mahrez (#26) receives a pass near the penalty area, cuts inside, and fires a low shot. The ball flies past the diving Manchester United goalkeeper David de Gea, who is in a blue patterned jersey, and into the net. "OH AND IT GOES! To propel them into the lead! Just what they wanted!" shouts the commentator as the stadium erupts in a massive, sustained roar and celebratory chants. The scoreboard confirms the goal at 56:12, updating to MCI 3-2 MUN. Mahrez celebrates with teammate Jack Grealish (#10), who embraces him, while replays from multiple angles, including a close-up behind the net and slow-motion, highlight the goal. A second commentator analyzes the goal, praising "what a good finish this is from Mahrez. He strikes it so cleanly and hits it with power. That's a great goal." On the sideline, Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola, in black, reacts with a clenched fist and animated celebration, mirroring the crowd's continued chants. The EA SPORTS LIVE logo remains visible. The match resumes at 57:27 with Manchester United in possession, player name graphics like Rashford (#10) and Grealish (#10) appearing, and pitch-side advertising boards displaying 'easports.com/fifa' and 'JOIN THE CLUB THIS JULY'. segments.integrated_caption: The Emirates FA Cup match between Manchester City and Manchester United is tied 3-3 at 81:37, with the commentator questioning if Manchester United has the 'guile to open up the defense.' Manchester United, in red jerseys, launches a sustained attack from the left flank, with the commentator noting they are 'holding onto the ball, waiting for the cavalry to arrive.' Fred is mentioned by the commentator as the ball is worked into the box, leading to Rashford (#10) taking a shot. The commentator exclaims, 'And now Rashford, can they forge ahead!' The shot beats the diving Manchester City goalkeeper, finding the back of the net, prompting an ecstatic 'Oh yes! The goal that might well be decisive! The goal that could get them the trophy!' from the commentator. The scoreboard updates to 3-4 in favor of Manchester United at 82:46, and the stadium crowd erupts with loud cheers and roars. Rashford celebrates with clenched fists and raised arms, joined by teammates Weghorst (#27) and Casemiro (#18) for a jubilant embrace. A close-up shows Rashford with a determined expression, acknowledging the animated crowd. A graphic overlay confirms the goal: '10 Marcus RASHFORD GOAL 83'. The commentator provides a replay analysis, stating, 'Well as the replay shows, this is all about his ability to find space in the box, which then makes the finish look so much easier. That's good play from him.' The scene transitions to a wide view of the pitch as teams reposition, with the scoreboard now displaying 'MANCHESTER CITY 3-4 MANCHESTER UTD' and 'RASHFORD 83' beneath, maintaining an electric atmosphere under the EA SPORTS LIVE broadcast overlay.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a whistle, signaling a dramatic turn in the simulated Emirates FA Cup match between Manchester City and Manchester United. Commentators exclaim, "Goal for Manchester United!" as Rashford (#10), in his red jersey, scores in the 83rd minute, making the score 3-4. He celebrates triumphantly, raising his arms, while the on-screen scoreboard updates. The commentator notes this "dramatic moment near the end" "might well be the decider," as intense crowd cheering erupts. However, the excitement is short-lived for United. Manchester City, in light blue, quickly counters. With five minutes left, the commentator anticipates "late excitement," and then shouts, "OH! A goal to level it up in the closing stages!" as Bernardo Silva (#20) dribbles past defenders and scores, equalizing the match at 4-4. Silva celebrates exuberantly, pointing skyward and being embraced by teammates, including Jack Grealish (#10). The stadium's large digital screen displays the updated 4-4 score amidst a massive, animated crowd, predominantly in red and blue, whose intense roars fill Wembley Stadium. A commentator later analyzes Silva's "great goal," noting how he "glides past the defender" and struck the ball with "such venom." Another whistle signals the restart, with the scoreboard showing 85:40. The match resumes with Manchester United's Weghorst (#27) and Rashford (#10) in possession, while Manchester City's Haaland (#9) and Silva (#20) press forward, all under the EA Sports Live broadcast overlay and pitch-side advertising for 'FIFA.GG' and 'easports.com/fc'. The commentators emphasize the tension, stating, "Who's going to claim the trophy? It's an open question here," and "you don't get too many scorelines like this one. 4-4. Silva!" as both teams vie for control in the charged final minutes.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the intense final moments of a simulated Emirates FA Cup match, Manchester City and Manchester United locked at 4-4 in stoppage time. From a high-angle view, Manchester United, in red, launches a final attack with Rashford (#10) and Dalot (#20) highlighted. Amidst the general stadium ambiance and crowd noise, a commentator describes the action, stating, "So the corner played into the box. Well, it has come to nothing." The ball is worked into the Manchester City penalty area, and as the crowd noise builds, a shot is taken. The commentator exclaims, "Well, the fans really want him to shoot, and the keeper's hand does the trick," as the Manchester City goalkeeper, in an orange jersey, dives to his right for a crucial save, followed by a brief scramble before the ball is cleared. A referee's whistle signals the end of regular time as the match clock advances into +3 minutes of stoppage time, with the scoreboard showing 'MCI 4-4 MUN'. The commentator confirms, "That is that after 90 minutes. They are level, and extra time is coming up." The camera pans across the pitch as players regroup amidst stadium ambiance and a faint game transition jingle. On-screen graphics display player names like Haaland (#9) for City and Fernandes (#8) for United, along with substitutions: Phillips replacing De Bruyne for Manchester City, and Sabitzer replacing Weghorst for Manchester United, announced by a stadium announcer. The packed stadium, filled with animated fans in red and blue, reacts to the drama. The segment transitions with upbeat electronic music, then the commentator sets the stage for the next period: "So back in business here, the first period of extra time, and it might well come down to mental fortitude, who wants this more." A full-screen graphic shows the final score and goal scorers, followed by detailed match statistics highlighting possession and shots. The EA SPORTS LIVE logo remains visible, alongside pitch-side advertising for 'Emirates FA Cup' and 'easports.com/fifa', all contributing to the electric atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The dramatic climax of the Emirates FA Cup final penalty shootout unfolds with Manchester City's Bernardo Silva (#20) taking a penalty, which a commentator notes "wasn't the greatest" as Manchester United goalkeeper David de Gea, praised for reacting "magnificently," saves it. The on-screen scoreboard updates, reflecting the tension. Manchester United's Antony (#21) then steps up, and as a commentator exclaims "And it's gone in!", he scores, giving United the lead. Jack Grealish (#10) keeps City's hopes alive by scoring, followed by Fred (#17) who successfully converts his penalty, putting United ahead once more. The tension escalates as City's goalkeeper Ederson (#31) takes and scores his penalty, equalizing the shootout. Fred then scores again, and the camera cuts to ecstatic Manchester United fans in red, their cheers and whistles audible amidst the general stadium atmosphere. With the score tied 5-5 after regular time, a commentator anticipates the decisive moment, stating "if he scores, they win." A Manchester United player, later identified as Rashford (#10) in the full-time graphic, takes the final penalty and scores, sealing a 6-5 victory on penalties for United. The stadium erupts in joy, with loud cheers and celebrations from the predominantly red crowd, as Rashford raises his arms triumphantly. Commentators declare, "Oh, that's it! The final act in the drama. One team jubilant, the other distraught," reflecting on the "great entertainment" and acknowledging United's deserved win while noting City's disappointment. The EA SPORTS LIVE broadcast overlay remains visible, alongside 'Emirates FA Cup' and 'FIFA' branding on pitch-side advertising boards, as the video concludes with a full-time graphic displaying the final score and goal scorers, including Rashford's 100th-minute goal, and players embracing.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of a dejected Manchester City player, his head down, as the on-screen scoreboard confirms a dramatic 5-5 draw after extra time and a penalty shootout. Commentators immediately set the scene, noting that "their football was brilliant" and that "the party [is] in full swing now," anticipating "quite the night of celebration here" amidst a swelling crowd noise and celebratory music. The camera then shifts to the jubilant Manchester United players in red 'TeamViewer' jerseys, celebrating their hard-fought victory. Antony (#21), Fred (#17), and Dalot (#20) are seen high-fiving and embracing, their elation palpable as the team gathers in a huddle on the pitch, surrounded by photographers and staff, with confetti beginning to fall. Commentators emphasize this as "special for everybody at the club," a "great moment for any young player," and "what you dream about when you start playing football." The scene transitions to the winners' podium under a 'WINNERS' arch, where Manchester United players, including McTominay (#39), Casemiro (#18), and Fred (#17), walk towards the Emirates FA Cup Trophy. As commentators announce, "And now for the trophy lift itself," followed by "The cup winners!", the captain triumphantly raises the large silver cup with red 'Emirates Fly Better' ribbons high above his head, accompanied by a loud crowd roar and a cascade of confetti. The goalkeeper, in a blue patterned jersey, joins the celebration, raising his arms in victory. Wembley Stadium is packed with an animated crowd in red and blue, their energy palpable, as the EA SPORTS LIVE broadcast overlay remains visible, capturing this "fantastic stuff" and what "it means to those players and the manager."

segments.integrated_caption: The dramatic Emirates FA Cup final at Wembley Stadium culminates in a triumphant celebration for Manchester United, who emerged victorious after a 5-5 draw followed by a penalty shootout. Manchester United players, clad in their red 'TeamViewer' jerseys, gather under a large white 'WINNERS' arch as confetti rains down. Commentators reflect on their journey, noting, "my goodness, they've taken the rough with the smooth," and now "they savor the moment." Loud crowd cheers and a whistle punctuate the air as players like McTominay (#39), Shaw (#23), Fred (#17), and Dalot (#20) celebrate wildly, embracing and lifting the ornate Emirates FA Cup Trophy high above their heads. The goalkeeper, David de Gea, in his blue patterned jersey, joins the jubilation, raising his arms in victory. A commentator gives "full marks to the entire team," stating they now "get the chance to exhale and enjoy." The camera pans across the jubilant team forming a tight huddle, while in the background, the predominantly red stadium crowd erupts in cheers, with fireworks bursting in the night sky, adding to the grandeur. Commentators praise the team's "brilliant" performance and the fans who "really got behind their team," playing "a big part in this win," describing it as "a match made in heaven between players and fans." Dejected Manchester City players in their light blue kits are visibly walking away from the pitch. The EA SPORTS LIVE broadcast overlay remains visible, along with pitch-side advertising boards displaying '#EmiratesFACup' and 'EA SPORTS LIVE', as the commentators predict these celebrations "are going to continue for quite some time" amidst the ongoing confetti and fireworks.
